raw_output.summary: The dominant simplicity problem in this codebase is structural: a three-layer indirection pattern (createLocalStorageHook factory → anonymous useHook constant → named wrapper function) is replicated across 12 nearly-identical files, producing ~120 lines of pure boilerplate with no differentiating logic. Collapsing this into the factory itself would delete most of those files. Secondary issues include a duplicate SearchResult interface across the real and demo search modules, and a type-cast workaround in PlaceholderSection that silently disables i18n key checking. The remaining findings are minor: a no-op useCallback wrapper and an unnecessary named intermediate in the hook factory.

findings.title: 12 near-identical localStorage hook wrapper files with zero added logic
findings.description: Every single-setting preference (auto-mark-read, date-mode, chat-position, layout, mascot, show-thumbnails, unread-indicator, etc.) follows a three-layer pattern: (1) createLocalStorageHook is called to produce an anonymous useHook, (2) that hook is immediately re-exported under a named function that does nothing but destructure the tuple into an object. For example, useAutoMarkRead is four lines that call useHook() and return { autoMarkRead, setAutoMarkRead }. All 12 files are structurally identical, adding ~120 lines of boilerplate with no logic differences whatsoever. The factory createLocalStorageHook already encapsulates all the real behaviour; the per-setting wrapper files provide only cosmetic renaming.
findings.recommendation: Extend createLocalStorageHook to return a named-object hook directly by accepting a valueName parameter, e.g. createLocalStorageHook<AutoMarkRead>('auto-mark-read', 'off', ['on','off'], 'autoMarkRead') producing { autoMarkRead, setAutoMarkRead }. Each preference then becomes a one-liner export and all 12 wrapper files can be deleted. Their tests can be consolidated into create-local-storage-hook.test.ts.

findings.title: Duplicate SearchResult interface defined independently in search.ts and search.demo.ts
findings.description: Both src/lib/search.ts and src/lib/search.demo.ts define a local interface SearchResult with identical shapes (id, title, url, feed_name, published_at). Because each file defines its own copy, the two implementations can drift silently — a field change in the real API requires remembering to also update the demo. Neither file exports the type, so consumers cannot reference it directly.
findings.recommendation: Define SearchResult once — either in shared/types (already imported elsewhere in the project) or a small src/lib/search-types.ts — and import it in both files. Export it so consumers can type their own variables against it.

findings.title: PlaceholderSection defeats i18n type safety with forced type casts
findings.description: In src/pages/settings/data-tab.tsx, PlaceholderSection accepts titleKey: string and descKey: string, then calls t(titleKey as 'settings.data') and t(descKey as 'settings.data') to silence the type checker. This completely bypasses the compile-time guarantee that t() only receives valid message keys. A typo in any call site will silently produce a missing-translation fallback at runtime. Casting to the single arbitrary key 'settings.data' is also semantically misleading.
findings.recommendation: Change titleKey and descKey to the actual i18n key union type that t() accepts (whatever MessageKey or equivalent the i18n module exports). This eliminates the casts and turns invalid key strings into compile errors.

findings.title: handleSummarize is a redundant useCallback wrapper around run
findings.description: In src/hooks/use-summarize.ts, const handleSummarize = useCallback(() => run(), [run]) creates a memoized function whose sole body is calling run(). If run is already a stable reference from useStreamingAI, handleSummarize is behaviourally identical to run and the wrapper adds a hook call plus an extra closure allocation for no benefit. If run is unstable, handleSummarize will also be unstable (it lists run as a dependency), so memoisation still provides nothing.
findings.recommendation: Remove the useCallback wrapper and expose run directly as handleSummarize via destructuring rename: const { ..., run: handleSummarize } = useStreamingAI(...). This removes an unnecessary hook call and a needless intermediate closure.

findings.title: getStored named helper in createLocalStorageHook is an unnecessary intermediate
findings.description: Inside createLocalStorageHook, the named inner function getStored() exists solely to be passed as the lazy initialiser to useStatenull(getStored). Its two-line body is not reused anywhere else. Naming it creates an extra identifier to track without adding clarity; an inline initialiser arrow would be equally readable and reduce one level of indirection.
findings.recommendation: Inline the logic directly into useState: useStatenull(() => { const stored = localStorage.getItem(key); return (stored !== null && allowed.has(stored)) ? (stored as T) : defaultValue }). Remove the separate getStored function declaration.
